title: Saving Files
drDigitalIntro: When you create something — like writing a letter, downloading a photo, or saving a webpage — you're saving a file to your computer. Most apps have a File menu with a Save option. When you save, your computer asks where you want to save it: maybe your Desktop, Documents, or a folder you created. Downloads also happens automatically — files you download from the internet go to your Downloads folder unless you tell them to go somewhere else.

playgroundTask.question: Which keyboard shortcut saves a file in most Mac apps?

drDigitalSuccess: Command + S — S for Save! It's one of the most important shortcuts to remember.
drDigitalHint: The shortcut follows the pattern: Command + the first letter of what you want to do.
